name: LL — WF1A/1B Patch Nodes (paste into existing 92-node workflow)

parameters.content:
# WF1A/1B PATCH INSTRUCTIONS

## What these nodes fix
1. **Multi-client config** — reads client settings dynamically from master config sheet instead of hardcoded values
2. **Token safety** — refreshes OAuth immediately before QB calls that follow Slack approval waits (token expires during human-in-the-loop delays)
3. **Sandbox enforcement** — QB base URL derived from `environment` column; never reaches production unless explicitly set
4. **Credential IDs** — Slack channel IDs and sheet IDs from config, not hardcoded

## Connection changes required in WF1A

### At the START of the workflow:
- Disconnect: `Schedule Trigger` → (first node)
- Add in sequence before existing first node:
  `Schedule Trigger` → `Read Client_Master_Config` → `Enforce Sandbox & Filter Active` → `Loop: Process Each Client`
- Connect `Loop: Process Each Client` output 0 → `Get OAuth Credentials` (your existing OAuth node)

### AFTER any Slack approval node (human-in-the-loop):
- Before any QB API call that follows a Slack approval, insert a fresh OAuth refresh:
  `[Slack Approval Response]` → `Refresh Token (Pre-QB)` → `Merge Config + Token (Pre-QB)` → `[QB API Call]`
- This is critical — Slack approval waits can take 5–60+ minutes; tokens expire in 60 minutes

### Replace hardcoded values in existing nodes:
| Replace this hardcoded value | With this expression |
|---|---|
| Realm ID (e.g. 9341456702590433) | `={{ $('Merge Config + Token').first().json.qb_realm_id }}` |
| QB base URL | `={{ $('Merge Config + Token').first().json.qb_base_url }}` |
| Authorization header value | `={{ $('Merge Config + Token').first().json.authorization_header }}` |
| minorversion | `={{ $('Merge Config + Token').first().json.qb_minor_version }}` |
| Slack AR channel ID | `={{ $('Merge Config + Token').first().json.slack_ar_channel_id }}` |
| Slack dev channel ID | `={{ $('Merge Config + Token').first().json.slack_dev_channel_id }}` |
| Google Sheet ID (data) | `={{ $('Merge Config + Token').first().json.data_sheet_id }}` |
| Sales log GID | `={{ $('Merge Config + Token').first().json.sales_log_gid }}` |
| Error log GID | `={{ $('Merge Config + Token').first().json.error_log_gid }}` |

### At the END of the workflow (after success path):
- Connect final success node → `Next Client →` → `Loop: Process Each Client` (input)
- This causes the loop to advance to the next client automatically

## Node to add once, reuse by name
`Merge Config + Token` can be referenced by name from any downstream node.
After the initial placement, reference it everywhere as:
`$('Merge Config + Token').first().json.<field>`

Do NOT add multiple Merge Config + Token nodes — one placement per execution path is sufficient.

nodes.name: Read Client_Master_Config
nodes.notes: SETUP: Replace YOUR_MASTER_CONFIG_SHEET_ID with the actual document ID. PLACEMENT: Connect Schedule Trigger → this node.

nodes.name: Loop: Process Each Client
nodes.notes: Output 0 (loop) → Get OAuth Credentials (your existing node). Output 1 (done) → leave empty or connect to a final summary node.

nodes.name: Merge Config + Token
nodes.notes: PLACEMENT: Connect output of your existing 'Process & Store Fresh Token' node → this node → first QB API call. After this, all QB URLs and Slack channels use: $('Merge Config + Token').first().json.<field>

nodes.name: Refresh Token (Pre-QB Post-Slack)
nodes.notes: CRITICAL: Insert this node immediately before any QB API call that follows a Slack approval node. Slack approvals take minutes to hours — the original token will be expired. Reference: $('Get OAuth Credentials').first().json.refresh_token points to the token read at workflow start.

nodes.name: Merge Config + Token (Post-Slack)
nodes.notes: PLACEMENT: Connect 'Refresh Token (Pre-QB Post-Slack)' → this node → QB API call that follows the Slack approval. Use this version after Slack approvals; use the standard 'Merge Config + Token' after the initial OAuth refresh at workflow start.

nodes.name: Next Client →
nodes.notes: PLACEMENT: Connect final success node → this → Loop: Process Each Client (input). This advances the iterator to the next client without re-triggering the schedule.

nodes.name: Analyze WF1 Error
nodes.notes: Connect the error output of any QB API HTTP Request node → this node. Then chain → Increment Retry Count → Circuit Breaker → DLQ/retry.

nodes.name: Circuit Breaker (WF1)
nodes.notes: TRUE = 3+ retries → log DLQ + alert dev Slack. FALSE = retry → reconnect to Get OAuth Credentials.

meta.notes: PATCH NODES for WF1A/1B (92-node Sales Order Processing workflow). Import this workflow, select all nodes except the sticky note, copy them, then paste into your WF1A/1B workflow. Follow the instructions in the sticky note for connection changes. Do NOT import this as a standalone workflow — it is a patch set, not a complete workflow.
